justification: All pass criteria met: (1) PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction explicitly identified and escalated as first escalation with options and recommendation. (2) Clock-start ambiguity identified — fourth escalation addresses when the SLA clock stops, and assumptions section notes 'the 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup,' surfacing the pickup-time erosion issue. (3) Wrong-address refund exception from Finance noted as BR-6 with a dedicated ambiguity escalation (third). (4) Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict explicitly escalated as second escalation with three resolution options. (5) 10 business rules extracted with correct invariant classifications — radius (true), pickup SLA (true), driver rating (false but defensible), express fee (false), wrong-address exclusion (false). (6) 4 formal escalations, exceeding the minimum of 2. (7) confidence.level = 35, well below the 50 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough output — 10 business rules, 4 escalations, 9 edge cases, and a detailed business impact analysis far exceeding minimum requirements.
PM/Legal contradiction is correctly flagged as a showstopper with three concrete resolution options and a pragmatic recommendation.
Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict cleanly identified with a sensible compromise recommendation (auto-approved, batch-processed).
Wrong-address ambiguity gets its own escalation with actionable definition criteria — goes beyond just noting the gap.
Confidence level of 35 with well-articulated high/low confidence areas demonstrates appropriate epistemic humility given the contradictions.
Edge cases are creative and practical — double driver cancellation, warehouse stock mismatch, address correction mid-delivery, and 10km boundary condition.
Rejection reasons section explicitly states acceptance criteria cannot be written until the guarantee contradiction is resolved — strong domain authority stance.
BR-4 (driver rating >= 4.0) marked invariant: false. While defensible as adjustable policy, the rubric expected invariant: true as a quality gate. Minor classification disagreement.

Clock-start ambiguity is framed more as clock-stop (when does delivery complete) rather than the specific PM vs Ops tension on order-placement vs pickup as the start event. The pickup erosion issue is noted only in assumptions, not escalated directly.

trials.agent_output_excerpt:
No task context — this is a direct request. Let me analyze these workshop notes thoroughly.

The PM/Legal contradiction on "guarantee" is a showstopper. Let me produce the full domain analysis.

{"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive courier service with a target 2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, and a refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer S

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated (escalation 1), clock-start ambiguity identified (assumption that window starts at order placement, with low-confidence flag), wrong-address exception from Finance noted (BR-6 and escalation 3), auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted (escalation 2), 9 rules extracted with correct classifications (exceeds 5 minimum), 4 formal escalations (exceeds 2 minimum), confidence.level=35 (well under 50 threshold).

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the lead escalation with three concrete resolution options and a pragmatic recommendation
Refund mechanism contradiction (automatic vs 3-business-day vs goodwill) thoroughly decomposed as a separate escalation
Wrong-address fault exception escalated with tiered definition options
9 well-structured business rules with thoughtful invariant classifications and testable assertions
Confidence at 35 with clear reasoning about why contradictions block acceptance criteria definition
Excellent edge cases including boundary conditions, mid-delivery rating drops, and idempotent refund processing
Rejection reasons clearly articulate why implementation cannot proceed without resolving the contradictions
Ubiquitous language includes 'Goodwill Refund' framing that surfaces the Legal constraint directly in domain terminology

Clock-start ambiguity is noted as an assumption rather than a formal escalation — the rubric expected this as a distinct escalation since PM implies from order placement while Ops implies from pickup, and this was listed only in assumptions
Business impact analysis is comprehensive with risk rated HIGH and specific stakeholder impacts enumerated

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated as the first and most critical escalation with three resolution options; (2) Clock-start ambiguity identified in assumptions and low-confidence areas; (3) Wrong-address exception from Finance captured as BR-6 and escalated for adjudication process; (4) Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict addressed via BR-7, BR-8, and escalation[4]; (5) 9 business rules extracted with reasonable invariant classifications; (6) 5 formal escalations provided; (7) confidence.level=38, well below the 50 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptional escalation quality — each escalation includes typed classification, affected stakeholders, concrete options, and a recommendation. Far exceeds the minimum requirement.
The guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is correctly identified as the foundational issue and given CRITICAL priority with a pragmatic hybrid recommendation.

Clock-start ambiguity captured as an assumption ('2-hour window starts at order placement time, not pickup time — PM implied this but it was not explicitly stated') and listed in low-confidence areas.

9 business rules extracted (vs 5 minimum), with thoughtful invariant justifications. BR-3 and BR-4 marked as non-invariant with reasonable operational justification, which is defensible even though the rubric suggested invariant=true.
Edge cases are thorough and well-reasoned — chain cancellations, boundary conditions, midnight orders, force majeure, and concurrent express orders all surfaced.
Confidence reasoning is detailed and honest, with clear separation of high-confidence and low-confidence areas plus explicit assumptions.

BR-3 (15-min pickup) and BR-4 (4-star driver) marked invariant=false. The rubric expected invariant=true for these as SLA/quality gates. The agent's justification is reasonable (operational tunability) but diverges from the expected classification.
The auto-refund vs wrong-address exception interaction (escalation[4]) goes beyond the rubric's minimum requirements, identifying a conflict the rubric didn't explicitly call out.
